title: The Spindle Dream Chamber

features.name: the-sleeping-spindle
features.desc-on-touch: A spindle dreams of patterns while it sleeps. The thread it produces carries the shape of its dreams. You trace the weave and find it remembers futures that never happened.

features.name: the-warp-repository
features.desc-on-touch: Threads held in arch-formation, tension just so. The warp remembers every pattern it has held. You pluck a thread and it sings in the voice of a loom long gone.

features.name: the-thread-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Wound threads pool like water. Different colours mean different dreams: indigo for waking, red for growing, gold for the dreams the spindles have about each other.

features.name: the-loom-collective
features.desc-on-touch: Looms that touch spindle to spindle without speaking. They coordinate through thread-pressure, through the language of tension and release. Their collaboration predates words.

features.name: the-pattern-inheritance
features.desc-on-touch: Each new spindle dreams of the patterns the old spindles wove. The dreams compound and evolve. What you see woven now has never been woven before, only ever dreamed.

inscription: the spindle dreams and the thread remembers; the loom weaves the dream into cloth; the cloth teaches the next spindle what to dream

file.name: Textile_Memory_Spectral_Analysis_Fragments.mp3
file.note: Threads recorded as sound. Each colour has a pitch. When played simultaneously, they form a chord that the spindles recognize as their own name.

reverie.title: REVERIE — teaching spindles to dream together
narr: She moves the threads in patterns you don't understand at first.
Then you see: the warp holds memory, the weft holds desire.
When you weave them together, something new learns to exist.
You add a thread of your own colour. It knows where to go.

exits.label: back toward where weavings still teach in silence
deeper into the chamber where dreaming becomes the loom itself
upward through thread-layers toward the finished cloth hanging in history
down into the thread-pile where all old patterns sleep, waiting to dream again
